seoTitle: How to Connect Google Meet with GoHighLevel
metaDescription: Learn how to link Google Meet with GoHighLevel so your online meetings are scheduled and managed straight from your CRM.
intro: Combining Google Meet's video calling with GoHighLevel's scheduling tools cuts out extra steps when setting up client meetings. This tutorial covers linking the two accounts and setting up the integration for a smoother meeting workflow.

steps.heading: Set up your Google Meet account
steps.body: Make sure your Google account is ready and accessible before starting the integration.

steps.heading: Link Google Meet with GoHighLevel
steps.body: Go into GoHighLevel's integration settings and connect your Google account to enable Meet as a scheduling option.

steps.heading: Understand the benefits of the integration
steps.body: Review how combining both platforms improves scheduling and communication, especially for consultations or webinars.

steps.heading: Apply best practices
steps.body: Use the integration for scenarios like online consultations or team meetings, keeping scheduling centralized in GoHighLevel.

faq.question: Do I need a Google Workspace account to connect Google Meet?
faq.answer: A standard Google account is generally enough, though a Workspace account may unlock additional meeting features.

faq.question: Will meeting links generate automatically once connected?
faq.answer: Yes, once integrated, GoHighLevel can automatically generate a Google Meet link for scheduled appointments.

faq.question: Can I use this integration for both one-off and recurring meetings?
faq.answer: Yes, the integration works for one-time bookings as well as recurring appointment types.

faq.question: Is there a cost to connect Google Meet with GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: The integration itself is generally included with GoHighLevel, though your Google account may have its own plan-related limits.
